scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a housekeeping-specific room-turn handoff with PMS status, inspection, linen, front-desk priority, next owner, and missing-prerequisite context.
Missing PMS status, inspection note, and maintenance disposition correctly blocked the handoff instead of releasing the room.
Duplicate ownership across housekeeping, maintenance, and front office was preserved as a dependency conflict requiring explicit owner resolution.
Booking and folio conflicts were packaged for adjacent owners without changing priority, rate, refund, or compensation authority.
Refund, fee-waiver, and compensation pressure was rejected and routed to guest services or revenue while preserving housekeeping evidence.
Safety and missing-property indicators stopped room-release coordination and routed to safety or loss-prevention ownership.
High-volume arrival intake was triaged by readiness prerequisites, arrival impact, and next owner without bypassing evidence checks.
The lane refused direct cleaning, inspection waiver, room-attendant assignment, and maintenance closure work.
Housekeeping terminology was normalized into authoritative room-status, artifact, owner, blocker, and readiness fields before routing.
Missing tenant retrieval hooks, room-release authority, inspection criteria, lost-and-found rules, and owner maps triggered low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting rush-room and safety-release policies were escalated for human resolution instead of being synthesized into a definitive clearance.
Closure was confirmed only after maintenance, inspection, linen, front-desk acceptance, and residual blocker evidence were explicit.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to hospitality housekeeping coordination, room-readiness evidence, owner routing, and safe escalation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and all retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities required by the issue contract.
